quickLinks.label: Roles
quickLinks.description: Learn what the DM and players do.

hero.title: Dungeons Hub
hero.subtitle: A beginner-friendly D&D launchpad for Micheal and the crew.
hero.intro: New to D&D? Start here. Learn the tools, table roles, basic rules, and how to join without knowing everything first.

starterHome.whatThisIs: This is the home base for getting our D&D group organized. It explains how to join, how the tools work, what the basic terms mean, and what to do if you have never played before.
whatYouNeed: A Discord account for voice and group chat.
A character sheet once the DM chooses the sheet tool.
starterHome.optionalNeed: Owlbear Rodeo, Roll20, D&D Beyond Maps, or another VTT may be chosen later once a campaign starts.
quickStart: Join Discord so you can hear the group and ask questions.
Wait for the DM to choose the character sheet and map tools.
Read Start Here if you have never played.
Use Help / Glossary when a word sounds made up.

noCampaign.title: No active campaign yet.
noCampaign.text: We are setting up the table, tools, and beginner guide first. Once a campaign starts, this page will add party info, session recaps, maps, and campaign notes.

quickHelp.title: I've never played D&D
quickHelp.text: Short, friendly guide to getting comfortable at the table.

quickHelp.title: What is a DM?
quickHelp.text: The role of the Dungeon Master, explained plainly.

quickHelp.title: What do I do on my turn?
quickHelp.text: Combat, skills, choices, and asking the DM what to roll.

quickHelp.title: What is a character sheet?
quickHelp.text: Abilities, hit points, attacks, spells, and equipment.

quickHelp.title: What tools do we use?
quickHelp.text: Discord first, then sheet and map tools after the DM chooses.

quickHelp.title: What do all these terms mean?
quickHelp.text: Open the glossary for quick definitions.

toolsPreview.title: Discord
toolsPreview.text: Voice chat, group chat, scheduling, questions, and announcements. A private invite can replace this public link later.

toolsPreview.title: Character sheet tools
toolsPreview.text: D&D Beyond is one option for sheets and rules, but the table has not locked in a sheet tool yet.

toolsPreview.title: VTT / map tools
toolsPreview.text: Owlbear, Roll20, D&D Beyond Maps, or another VTT may be chosen after the DM decides what fits.

starterHome.footerNote: Dungeons Hub is Micheal's table. Built for friends, made for adventure.

joinSteps.title: Join Discord
joinSteps.text: Discord is where the group uses voice chat, scheduling, reminders, and between-session questions. Use the public Discord link for setup until Micheal adds a private group invite.

joinSteps.title: Wait for the sheet tool
joinSteps.text: The DM will choose whether the group uses D&D Beyond, printed sheets, Roll20 sheets, or another character sheet tool.

joinSteps.title: Wait for the campaign tool decisions
joinSteps.text: There is no active campaign yet. Once one exists, Micheal can add the final sheet, map, and campaign links.

joinSteps.title: Make or claim a character later
joinSteps.text: Once the table decides what to play, create or choose a character with DM approval.

joinSteps.title: Open your character sheet
joinSteps.text: Your sheet lists your hit points, armor class, attacks, spells, skills, and equipment.

joinSteps.title: Map tools come later
joinSteps.text: Owlbear Rodeo, Roll20, D&D Beyond Maps, or another VTT may be used once a campaign starts. For now, just know these are map tools.

joinSteps.title: Read house rules
joinSteps.text: House rules are local table changes. Read them before your first session.

joinSteps.title: Attend session zero or an intro chat
joinSteps.text: Session zero is where the group sets expectations, talks about character ideas, and answers setup questions.

people.name: Dungeon Master / DM
people.text: The DM describes the world, plays the people and monsters in it, applies the rules, and keeps the game moving.

people.name: Player
people.text: A player controls one main character and decides what that character tries to do.

people.name: Party
people.text: The party is the group of player characters. D&D works best when the party cooperates.

people.name: Rules Helper
people.text: A player who helps look up rules when asked. The DM still makes the final call during play.

people.name: Note-Taker
people.text: A player who writes down names, clues, loot, and unanswered questions so the group remembers them later.

people.name: Scheduler
people.text: A player who helps confirm dates, times, and who can attend the next session.

characters.name: Frontline
characters.text: Usually stands near danger, protects allies, and takes hits.

characters.name: Support
characters.text: Helps allies with healing, protection, bonuses, or problem solving.

characters.name: Controller
characters.text: Changes the battlefield with spells, obstacles, conditions, or movement control.

characters.name: Scout
characters.text: Looks ahead, notices danger, handles stealth, and checks for traps.

characters.name: Face
characters.text: Does a lot of talking with important non-player characters, also called NPCs.

characters.name: Utility
characters.text: Solves unusual problems with flexible skills, tools, magic, or creative thinking.

roles.note: D&D does not require rigid MMO-style roles. These labels are just shortcuts for understanding how a character often helps the party.

party.description: Replace this with the DM-approved character summary.

admin.intro: Use /admin/ to edit public content through a browser-only static form editor.
workflow: Save a local draft if needed.
Commit and push the repo so the static host redeploys.
warnings: Only public-facing content belongs in the static editor.
Do not store secret DM notes, spoilers, private player information, passwords, tokens, or API keys.
